title: shorea trapezifolia
intro: Shorea trapezifolia, also known as the Burmese mahogany or Trapezifolia Shorea, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Shorea trapezifolia include its potential to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often dried and made into infusions, decoctions, or used in traditional remedies such as tonics and poultices.
